annotations.label: Centroid (general definition)

anchor.snippet: the mean position of all the points in the figure

annotations.note: Mathlib formalizes only the discrete `Finset.centroid` (mean of finitely many points), not the general 'mean position of all points in a figure'.

annotations.label: Barycenter / center of mass

anchor.snippet: one often assumes uniform mass density

annotations.note: `Finset.centerMass` is the discrete weighted barycenter; continuous uniform-density barycenter of a figure is not formalized.

annotations.label: Center of gravity

anchor.snippet: a center of gravity can be defined as the weighted mean of all points

annotations.note: Mathlib has the discrete weighted-mean `Finset.centerMass`; the gravity-field / continuous version is not present.

annotations.label: Geographical center

anchor.snippet: the centroid of a radial projection of a region of the Earth's surface to sea level

annotations.note: No geography-specific centroid construction is in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Centroid of convex object lies inside

anchor.snippet: The geometric centroid of a convex object always lies in the object

annotations.note: Mathlib proves the discrete centroid of a finite point set lies in its convex hull, but not the analogous statement for a general convex body's geometric centroid.

annotations.label: Centroid is fixed point of symmetry group

anchor.snippet: it is a fixed point of all isometries in its symmetry group

annotations.note: No theorem in Mathlib asserts that the centroid is preserved by every isometry of a figure's symmetry group.

annotations.label: Centroid of a parallelogram

anchor.snippet: the centroid of a parallelogram is the meeting point of its two diagonals

annotations.note: Mathlib has no `Parallelogram` structure nor a theorem identifying its centroid with the diagonals' intersection.

annotations.label: Centroid undefined under translational symmetry

anchor.snippet: the centroid of an object with translational symmetry is undefined

annotations.note: No corresponding lemma in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Centroid of a triangle (medians)

anchor.snippet: The centroid of a triangle is the intersection of the three medians of the triangle

annotations.note: Stated in arbitrary dimension (1 < n): the unique point lying on every median of a simplex is the centroid; specializes to the triangle case.

annotations.label: Centroid of a finite set of points

anchor.section: Of a finite set of points
anchor.snippet: The centroid of a finite set of

annotations.note: `Finset.centroid` is exactly the affine combination with uniform weights 1/#s.

annotations.label: Minimizes sum of squared distances

anchor.section: Of a finite set of points
anchor.snippet: This point minimizes the sum of squared Euclidean distances between itself and each point in the set

annotations.note: No Mathlib lemma identifies the centroid as the minimizer of the sum of squared Euclidean distances to a finite point set.

annotations.label: Centroid by geometric decomposition

anchor.snippet: can be computed by dividing it into a finite number of simpler figures

annotations.note: The decomposition formula for computing centroids of figures is not formalized.

annotations.label: Square, triangle, and circular hole decomposition

anchor.snippet: the figure below (a) is easily divided into a square and a triangle

annotations.note: No such worked example exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Generalization to higher dimensions

anchor.snippet: The same formula holds for any three-dimensional objects

annotations.note: Mathlib does not formalize the geometric-decomposition formula for centroids of 3D objects.

annotations.label: Centroid by integral formula (vector)

anchor.snippet: can also be computed by the vector formula

annotations.note: No definition of a region's centroid as ∫x dA / ∫dA appears in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Coordinate-wise centroid formula

anchor.snippet: the coordinate-wise formula for the centroid is defined as

annotations.note: Continuous coordinate-wise integral centroid formula is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Barycentric coordinates of plane figure

anchor.snippet: For a plane figure, in particular, the barycentric coordinates are

annotations.note: Mathlib has abstract `AffineBasis.coord` barycentric coordinates but no integral barycentric formula for a plane region.

annotations.label: Centroid of bounded region between two curves

anchor.snippet: of a region bounded by the graphs of the continuous functions

annotations.note: No formalization of the centroid of a region between two graphs.

annotations.label: Integraph and Green's theorem

anchor.snippet: The mathematical principle involved is a special case of Green's theorem

annotations.note: Mathlib lacks both the integraph centroid construction and Green's-theorem-based centroid formula.

annotations.label: Centroid of an L-shaped object

anchor.snippet: This is a method of determining the centroid of an L-shaped object

annotations.note: No such worked example exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Centroid of a triangle (medians and 2:1 ratio)

anchor.snippet: The centroid of a triangle is the point of intersection of its medians

annotations.note: Combined with `Affine.Simplex.eq_centroid_of_forall_mem_median`, this gives the medians' intersection and the 2:1 distance ratio for triangles.

annotations.label: Centroid in barycentric coordinates

anchor.snippet: The centroid is therefore at

annotations.note: `centroidWeights` assigns equal weight 1/n to each vertex, which is the (1/3,1/3,1/3) barycentric statement for a triangle, but no triangle-specific lemma stating this in barycentric form is present.

annotations.label: Centroid in trilinear coordinates

anchor.snippet: In trilinear coordinates the centroid can be expressed in any of these equivalent ways

annotations.note: Trilinear coordinates are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Centroid as physical center of mass

anchor.snippet: The centroid is also the physical center of mass if the triangle is made from a uniform sheet of material

annotations.note: Discrete `centroid = centerMass` is proved; the physical center-of-mass interpretation for a uniform triangular lamina is not separately formalized.

annotations.label: Spieker center for perimeter-distributed mass

anchor.snippet: the center of mass lies at the Spieker center

annotations.note: No declaration containing 'Spieker' exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Area via centroid distance

anchor.snippet: The area of the triangle is

annotations.note: No area-via-centroid-distance formula is in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Centroid on Euler line

anchor.snippet: A triangle's centroid lies on its Euler line between its orthocenter

annotations.note: Mathlib proves the collinearity formula H = 3(G − O) + O directly but does not define `EulerLine` as a named object.

annotations.label: Relation with incenter and nine-point center

anchor.snippet: In addition, for the incenter

annotations.note: Mathlib has incenter and nine-point-circle, but no centroid distance relations among them are formalized.

annotations.label: Isogonal conjugate of centroid

anchor.snippet: The isogonal conjugate of a triangle's centroid is its symmedian point

annotations.note: Neither isogonal conjugate nor symmedian point are in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Medians bisect triangle area

anchor.snippet: Any of the three medians through the centroid divides the triangle's area in half

annotations.note: Triangle-area lemmas in Mathlib do not include the median-bisection property.

annotations.label: Sum of squared distances from a point

anchor.snippet: the sum of the squared distances of

annotations.note: Leibniz-style identity relating sum of squared distances to the centroid is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Sum of squares of sides equals three times sum from centroid

anchor.snippet: The sum of the squares of the triangle's sides equals three times the sum of the squared distances of the centroid from the vertices

annotations.note: No such identity is formalized.

annotations.label: Centroid maximizes product of directed distances

anchor.snippet: A triangle's centroid is the point that maximizes the product of the directed distances of a point from the triangle's sidelines

annotations.note: No such optimization characterization of the centroid is in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Midpoint identity for any point in plane

anchor.snippet: be the midpoints of segments

annotations.note: Snippet trimmed to remove [MATH] token.

annotations.label: Centroid of a polygon

anchor.snippet: The centroid of a non-self-intersecting closed polygon defined by

annotations.note: `Mathlib/Geometry/Polygon/Basic.lean` defines `Polygon` but no centroid construction for it.

annotations.label: Signed area via shoelace formula

anchor.snippet: is the polygon's signed area

annotations.note: The shoelace formula for polygon signed area is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Polygon centroid vs vertex centroid

anchor.snippet: The centroid of a non-triangular polygon is not the same as its vertex centroid

annotations.note: No comparison of polygon centroid and vertex centroid in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Centroid of a cone or pyramid

anchor.snippet: The centroid of a cone or pyramid is located on the line segment that connects the apex to the centroid of the base

annotations.note: Cones and pyramids are not present as geometric figures in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Medians and bimedians of tetrahedron concur at centroid

anchor.section: Of a tetrahedron and n -dimensional simplex
anchor.snippet: These seven line segments all meet at the centroid of the tetrahedron

annotations.note: Concurrence of the four medians at the centroid is proven for all simplices with 1 < n; bimedians are not formalized as such.

annotations.label: Tetrahedron medians divided in 3:1 ratio at centroid

anchor.section: Of a tetrahedron and n -dimensional simplex
anchor.snippet: The medians are divided by the centroid in the ratio

annotations.note: `Affine.Simplex.dist_point_centroid` gives the n:1 ratio in arbitrary dimension; n=3 specializes to 3:1 for tetrahedra.

annotations.label: Tetrahedron centroid as midpoint of Monge point and circumcenter

anchor.section: Of a tetrahedron and n -dimensional simplex
anchor.snippet: The centroid of a tetrahedron is the midpoint between its Monge point and circumcenter

annotations.note: The general Monge-point/circumcenter/centroid relation for n-simplices is in Mathlib; the specialized 'midpoint' phrasing for tetrahedra is not separately stated.

annotations.label: Centroid of an n-dimensional simplex

anchor.section: Of a tetrahedron and n -dimensional simplex
anchor.snippet: These results generalize to any

annotations.note: `Affine.Simplex.centroid` is the centroid of the simplex's vertices for arbitrary n.

annotations.label: Simplex centroid as center of mass

anchor.section: Of a tetrahedron and n -dimensional simplex
anchor.snippet: The geometric centroid coincides with the center of mass if the mass is uniformly distributed over the whole simplex

annotations.note: Discrete equivalence of `centroid` and `centerMass` is proved; uniform mass distribution over a continuous simplex is not formalized.

annotations.label: Centroid of a solid hemisphere

anchor.snippet: The centroid of a solid hemisphere

annotations.note: No hemisphere centroid computation appears in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Centroid of a hollow hemisphere

anchor.snippet: The centroid of a hollow hemisphere

annotations.note: No hollow-hemisphere centroid computation appears in Mathlib.
